etymology.description: Depicts human remains -> variant form of 歹(歹) (dǎi, "bad; death")
etymology.interpretation: The character 歺 evolved as a variant of the character 歹(歹) (dǎi, "bad; death"), depicting human remains. The top component in characters like 餐(餐) (cān, "meal") historically retained the intermediate form of 歺, showcasing its connection to the idea of decay or death.

examples.chinese: 这是一种罕见的古字(這是一種罕見的古字)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè shì yī zhǒng hǎnjiàn de gǔ zì.
examples.english: This is a rare ancient character.

examples.chinese: “歺”这个字(“歺”這個字)在现代汉语中很少使用(在現代漢語中很少使用)。
examples.pinyin: “È” zhège zì zài xiàndài hànyǔ zhōng hěn shǎo shǐyòng.
examples.english: The character “歺” is rarely used in modern Chinese.

examples.chinese: 我们老师介绍了“歺”字的起源(我們老師介紹了“歺”字的起源)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen lǎoshī jièshàole “è” zì de qǐyuán.
examples.english: Our teacher introduced the origin of the character “歺”.
